Your Journey Awaits

Plan Your Visit

"The boat, meals, and crew are included.
The rest is up to you."

Length of Stay

5+ nights

Minimum five nights required. Longer stays are always welcome — the Nile rewards those who linger.

Number of Guests

4 – 50

The VEDA fleet hosts all types of groups and families, from intimate couples to large retreats.

Full Flexibility

Your pace

Where you go and what you do is completely flexible. We are happy to provide recommendations tailored to your energy.

Daily Itinerary

Luxor to
Aswan

The VEDA crew guides you to the most impressive and high-energy places along the Nile.

Day 1

Day 1

Luxor

Luxor offers one of the most exquisite ancient treasures in the world. The Valley of Kings, the legendary temple of Queen Hatshepsut, and Karnak Temple are some of the many jewels to explore in the magic city of Luxor.

Day 2

Day 2

Karnak · Esna

Esna is a busy little farming town located 55 km south of Luxor, on the west bank of the Nile. The Temple of Khnum, located 9 metres below street level, is one of the highlights. The lively market in Esna is a perfect place to immerse in real Egyptian vibes.

Day 3

Day 3

Crossing the Lock

The Esna Lock connects Esna to Aswan with an elevation difference of approximately eight metres. Crossing the lock is always a very loud and joyful experience — a scenic photo spot for Nile travellers.

Day 4

Day 4

Esna · Edfu

Catapult yourself back in time. Watch the countryside with little mud brick houses and observe the crops growing on the fertile Nile land. These paradisiacal views are ideal to relax, leave your worries behind, and become one with nature.

Day 5

Day 5

Silsila

During the 18th dynasty, the Egyptians started using sandstone instead of limestone — Silsila became a vital stone source for famous ancient temples. Next to its powerful energy and ancient vibes, Silsila is uniquely serene because regular boats cannot dock here.

Day 6

Day 6

Kom Ombo

Kom Ombo is a temple dedicated to Sobek (the crocodile god) and Haroeris (the winged god of medicine). Crocodiles were worshipped along the Nile and often buried with Pharaohs as protectors in the afterlife. The temple is also known for ancient surgical and dental tools depicted on its walls.

Day 7

Day 7

Aswan

Aswan's most sacred temple is Philae — considered the most precious of all Egypt's temples. Sitting on an island in the middle of the Nile, it is devoted to Isis, Goddess of motherhood. Philae is the last ancient temple built in classical Egyptian architectural style.

Energy Treatments

Connect with
mind, body & soul

Make your trip complete and book one of our energy-boosting treatments.

Meditation

Deepen your awareness. Combines ancient temple healing with guided instruction.

Yoga

Sunrise classes on the rooftop deck. Step fully into your body.

Massage

After visiting the ancient healing temples, your body will be grateful. Integrative and restorative.

Special Diets

Gluten-free, vegetarian, vegan, and detox options — all prepared with organic, local ingredients.

Wellness

Gym sessions, walks, runs, bike rides, and healing techniques tailored to your energy.

Ready to plan your journey?

Contact us and we'll create a personalised itinerary around your group, dates, and intentions.
